title: ECG Arrhythmia Classification (PWDR)
sub_domain: Cardiac electrical signal feature recovery with arrhythmia categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 PWDR Principle. Wraps L1-388 Blind Source Separation (existing in v1 genesis as the closest sibling biomedical-signal-processing core; ECG processing involves separating cardiac signal from baseline drift + noise; future dedicated ECG-feature-recovery core could be authored separately). Threshold function applies AAMI ANSI/AAMI EC57 standard arrhythmia classes per beat: {N normal, S supraventricular ectopic, V ventricular ectopic, F fusion, Q unclassifiable / paced}. Universal in cardiology, cardiac monitoring, FDA-cleared wearable ECG (Apple Watch, KardiaMobile, Zio Patch). UTSW Cardiology Department + ICU monitoring use.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on AAMI EC57 arrhythmia categorical {N, S, V, F, Q}

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Per-beat: AAMI_class = step on (RR_interval, QRS_morphology_template_distance, P_wave_presence, QRS_width_ms): N (normal sinus) if RR within 60-100 bpm + normal QRS template + P-wave preceding; S (supraventricular ectopic) if RR < 80% prior + abnormal P-wave or no P-wave; V (ventricular ectopic) if QRS_width > 120 ms + abnormal QRS template + no preceding P; F (fusion) if QRS template intermediate; Q (unclassifiable) if pacemaker spikes or unreadable.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: ECG signal feature recovery: R-peak detection (timing), QRS morphology template distance, P-wave detection / classification, beat-to-beat RR interval — recovered from blind-source-separated ECG signal via L1-388
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: RR interval and QRS width are continuous on R-peak / QRS-onset detection; class boundaries form measure-zero hyperplanes at threshold values. Smoothed-classification globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.05 per millisecond of detection error.

related_principles: L1-388 (Blind Source Separation — sibling biomedical-signal-processing core, physics_core_principle; note imperfect fit, dedicated ECG-feature-recovery core would be ideal)
L1-068 (EEG — sibling neuro-electrical signal modality, informational)
L1-067 (MEG — sibling magnetic neural signal, informational)

E.description: ECG Arrhythmia Classification (PWDR): wraps L1-388-style biomedical signal processing with established AAMI EC57 standard arrhythmia rules. Stage 1 (analytical, sibling to L1-388): from multi-lead or single-lead ECG, separate cardiac signal from baseline drift + powerline interference + EMG via blind source separation (ICA / NMF / wavelet); detect R-peaks via Pan-Tompkins or learned filter; extract per-beat features (RR interval, QRS morphology template, P-wave presence, QRS width, ST elevation/depression). Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply AAMI EC57 decision rules to classify each beat. Difficulty tier delta = 3.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (sibling to L1-388): raw_ECG -> separated_cardiac + features {R_peak_times, QRS_morphology, P_wave, RR}. Stage 2: AAMI_class = step(features per AAMI EC57 thresholds).
E.world_state_x: Per-beat AAMI class (N/S/V/F/Q); optionally per-recording clinical rhythm classification (NSR/AF/AFL/etc.).
E.observation_y: ECG signal: 1-12 leads, typically 250-500 Hz sampling, 5-second to 24-hour duration.

G.dag: L.ecg_acquisition -> L.bandpass_filter -> L.blind_source_separation -> L.r_peak_detection -> L.qrs_feature_extraction -> L.aami_threshold_classifier -> int.temporal
coupling_constraints: features_to_class: RR + QRS morphology + P-wave features are jointly observed and jointly determine AAMI class via decision tree

W.regime: Existence inherited from L1-388. Uniqueness conditional on adequate SNR (typical 20-30 dB) and clean lead placement. Stability dominated by motion_artifact_wearable for consumer ECG. Joint Hadamard well-posedness established by Pan-Tompkins 1985 (foundational R-peak detection), AAMI 1998 EC57 standard, Hannun 2019 (deep-learning ECG arrhythmia detection at cardiologist level), Attia 2019 (Apple Heart Study), Perez 2019 (smartwatch AF screening).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + signal-processing [Pan-Tompkins R-peak + ICA / wavelet template] + categorical-readout [AAMI EC57 classifier] | end-to-end deep neural [DeepECG, Hannun-Andrew Stanford CNN]
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-388 separation accuracy. Misclassification probability bound: Pr(AAMI_hat != AAMI_true) <= C * |feature_error| / d(features, AAMI_boundary).
C.complexity: O(N_samples * log(N_samples)) for stage 1 + O(N_beats) for stage 2

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original: 'Classify ECG into arrhythmia types'. Recast: 'Recover R-peaks + QRS morphology + RR features via signal processing (L1-388 sibling); apply AAMI EC57 decision rules per beat'. Feasible because cardiac electrical activity has well-defined biopotential forward model and AAMI rules are deterministic.

joint_well_posedness_references: Pan J, Tompkins WJ (1985) A real-time QRS detection algorithm. IEEE Trans Biomed Eng 32(3):230-236.
AAMI / ANSI EC57 (1998, revised 2012) Testing and reporting performance results of cardiac rhythm and ST segment measurement algorithms.
Hannun AY et al. (2019) Cardiologist-level arrhythmia detection and classification in ambulatory electrocardiograms using a deep neural network. Nat Med 25:65-69.
Attia ZI et al. (2019) An artificial intelligence-enabled ECG algorithm for the identification of patients with atrial fibrillation during sinus rhythm. Lancet 394(10201):861-867.
Perez MV et al. (2019) Large-scale assessment of a smartwatch to identify atrial fibrillation (Apple Heart Study). NEJM 381(20):1909-1917.
Moody GB, Mark RG (2001) The impact of the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database. IEEE Eng Med Biol Mag 20(3):45-50.

distinctness_audit.advisory_label: Distinct (sibling-physics-core PWDR; future dedicated ECG core recommended)
distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-388 BSS recovers separated source signals from mixed observations. L1-528 specializes to ECG with cardiac-specific features (R-peak, QRS morphology, P-wave) and adds AAMI EC57 classification. Different solution_space (1D AAMI class per beat vs separated source signals); adds L.r_peak_detection, L.qrs_feature_extraction, L.aami_threshold_classifier primitives. d_principle ~ 0.50.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal in cardiology — every ECG (clinical 12-lead, Holter, telemetry, wearable) is reviewed for arrhythmia. AAMI EC57 is the FDA-recognized testing standard for arrhythmia detection algorithms. UTSW Cardiology Department + ICU monitoring. FDA-cleared autonomous AI: Apple Watch ECG, KardiaMobile (AliveCor), Zio Patch (iRhythm) — all PWDR-shaped. Deep learning systems (Hannun et al. 2019) achieve cardiologist-level performance on Stanford / Geisinger benchmarks.
